file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the HP Smart Dashboard page of the application. Contains all UI element locators for the promotional page, subscription management, printer options, and footer navigation links. Provides element definitions for HP Instant Ink enrollment, plan updates, printer management, and footer links using Playwright selector strategies.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements and locators for the HP Smart Dashboard page. Provides locators for promotional page elements, subscription state indicators, HP Instant Ink enrollment links, printer management options, plan update controls, and footer navigation links. This page object follows the BasePageObject pattern where locators are stored in the self.elements namespace. Used for interacting with the HP Smart dashboard after user authentication.

locators.ui_element_description: Sign up now button on the promotional page that initiates the HP Instant Ink enrollment process. This is the primary call-to-action button for getting started with the service.
Background container element of the promotional page. Uses a partial ID match with the ^= operator to match IDs starting with 'promotionalPage__promotional-page-container-parity'.

Disclaimer container on the promotional page that displays legal disclaimers and terms information. Uses partial class match to find elements with class starting with 'promotionalPage__disclaimer-container'.
Link to Keypoint Intelligence information related to ink savings. This is a footnote link that provides additional details about savings claims.
Shipping and billing management page container. This element contains the React component for managing shipping addresses and billing information.
Footer link to HP.com main website. Located in the page footer navigation section.
Footer link to HP Wireless Print Center. Provides access to wireless printing setup and support resources.
Footer link to HP Help Center. Navigates users to support and help documentation.
Footer link to Terms of Use page. Displays the legal terms and conditions for using HP Instant Ink service.
Footer link to HP Privacy Statement. Provides access to HP's privacy policy and data handling practices.
Footer link to Your Privacy Choices page. Allows users to manage their privacy preferences and opt-out options.

Subscription state indicator element that displays the current status of the HP Instant Ink subscription. Uses a compound selector to match either subscription-state or ii-enrollment-status-printers-pp test IDs.
Link to enroll in HP Instant Ink service. Uses text-based selector to find the element containing the exact text 'Enroll in HP Instant Ink'.
Printer options control element that expands or collapses printer management options. Uses aria-controls attribute to find elements controlling either printerOptions or printer-options-data sections.
Link to update the current HP Instant Ink plan. Allows users to change their subscription plan or page allowance.
Link to remove a printer from the HP Instant Ink subscription. Allows users to unenroll a printer from the service.
Title element of the Printers page. Displays the heading for the printer management section.

Print plans promotional page container. Uses partial ID match to find the promotional page container element. This is the same selector as promotional_page_background, suggesting it may be used in different contexts.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the DashboardHPSmartPage by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page) to set up the page instance. Then defines 28 UI element locators as attributes on the self.elements object. These locators include the signup_now_button for enrollment, promotional_page_background and promotional_page_disclaimer for the promotional page layout, keypoint_intelligence_link for savings information, shipping_billing_page for payment management, six footer navigation links (footer_hp_com_link, footer_wireless_print_center_link, footer_help_center_link, footer_terms_of_use_link, footer_hp_privacy_link, footer_hp_your_privacy_choices_link), subscription_state for enrollment status, enroll_hp_instant_ink_link for enrollment, printer_options for printer management controls, update_plan_link for plan changes, remove_printer_link for printer removal, printers_page_title for the page heading, and print_plans_page for the promotional container. All locators use CSS selectors with data-testid attributes, partial ID matches, class selectors, or text-based selectors for reliable element identification.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This page object follows the BasePageObject pattern where all locators are stored as attributes on self.elements rather than directly on self. This is a different pattern from typical page objects but provides namespace organization. The page contains a comprehensive set of locators for the HP Smart dashboard including promotional content, subscription management, printer options, and footer navigation. Many locators use data-testid attributes which are the most reliable selector strategy. Some locators use partial matches with ^= operator for dynamic IDs. The class has only the constructor method and no action or verification methods, suggesting that interactions with these elements are handled by test scripts directly or by methods in the BasePageObject parent class. There are 28 locators defined covering enrollment, subscription state, printer management, plan updates, and footer links.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the Dashboard Side Menu navigation component. Contains all side menu UI element locators and interaction methods for navigating between different sections of the HP Instant Ink dashboard including Overview, Update Plan, Print History, Shipment Tracking, Account settings, Shipping & Billing, Notifications, and Printers. Handles both desktop and mobile menu layouts with automatic expansion logic.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements and navigation methods for the dashboard side menu component. Provides locators for all menu items including Instant Ink submenu items (Overview, Update Plan, Print History, Shipment Tracking) and Account submenu items (Shipping & Billing, Notifications). Contains intelligent click methods that automatically handle parent menu expansion for nested menu items and mobile layout menu expansion. Handles responsive design where the side menu may be collapsed in mobile layouts and needs to be expanded before clicking menu items.

locators.ui_element_description: Toggle button to expand or collapse the side menu in mobile layout. This hamburger menu icon appears in responsive mobile views where the side menu is initially hidden.
HP Instant Ink parent menu item in the side navigation. This is an expandable menu item that contains submenu items like Overview, Update Plan, Print History, and Shipment Tracking.
Overview menu item under the HP Instant Ink section. Navigates to the subscription overview page showing current plan details and usage.
Update Plan menu item under the HP Instant Ink section. Navigates to the plan update page where users can change their subscription plan.
Print and Payment History menu item under the HP Instant Ink section. Navigates to the history page showing past printing activity and payment transactions.
Shipment Tracking menu item under the HP Instant Ink section. Navigates to the shipment tracking page showing ink cartridge delivery status.
Account parent menu item in the side navigation. This is an expandable menu item that contains submenu items like Shipping & Billing and Notifications.
Shipping & Billing submenu item under the Account section. Navigates to the page for managing shipping addresses and billing payment methods.
Notifications submenu item under the Account section. Navigates to the notifications preferences page where users can manage email and communication settings.
Printers menu item in the side navigation. Navigates to the printers management page showing enrolled printers and their status.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the DashboardSideMenuPage by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page). Sets the default timeout for page operations to 30000 milliseconds (30 seconds) using self.page.set_default_timeout(30000). Then defines 10 UI element locators as attributes on the self.elements object for all side menu navigation items. These include expand_side_menu_in_mobile_layout for the mobile menu toggle, instant_ink_menu_link for the HP Instant Ink parent menu, overview_menu_link, update_plan_menu_link, print_history_menu_link, and shipment_tracking_menu_link for Instant Ink submenu items, my_account_menu_link for the Account parent menu, shipping_billing_submenu_link and notifications_submenu_link for Account submenu items, and printers_menu_link for the Printers section. All locators use data-testid attributes for reliable element identification.

parameters.name: parent_element
parameters.description: The parent menu element that needs to be expanded first

functions.what_it_does: Attempts to click a submenu target element with automatic parent menu expansion if needed. First tries to click the target_element directly. If the click fails (likely because the submenu is not visible), it catches the exception, clicks the parent_element to expand the parent menu section, and then clicks the target_element again. This handles the case where submenu items are hidden until their parent menu is expanded. The method uses a try-except block to handle the initial click failure gracefully.

parameters.name: target_element
parameters.description: The Instant Ink submenu element to click

functions.what_it_does: Attempts to click an Instant Ink submenu element with automatic handling of both mobile menu expansion and parent menu expansion. First tries to click the target_element directly with a 2000ms timeout and returns immediately if successful. If that fails, it checks if the mobile menu toggle (expand_side_menu_in_mobile_layout) is visible, and if so, clicks it to expand the side menu in mobile layout, then waits for the target_element to become visible with a 30000ms timeout. Both of these operations are wrapped in try-except blocks to handle cases where they're not needed. Finally, it calls self._click_with_parent_expansion(target_element, self.instant_ink_menu_link) to handle expanding the Instant Ink parent menu if the submenu item is still not clickable. This method handles three levels of menu visibility: mobile menu expansion, parent menu expansion, and the final target click.

parameters.name: target_element
parameters.description: The Account submenu element to click

functions.what_it_does: Clicks an Account submenu element with automatic parent menu expansion. This is a convenience wrapper that calls self._click_with_parent_expansion(target_element, self.my_account_menu_link) to handle clicking submenu items under the Account section. It ensures the Account parent menu is expanded before attempting to click the target submenu item.

Clicks the Overview menu item in the side navigation to navigate to the subscription overview page. Calls self._click_with_instant_ink_expansion(self.overview_menu_link) which automatically handles mobile menu expansion and Instant Ink parent menu expansion if needed before clicking the Overview link.

Clicks the Update Plan menu item in the side navigation to navigate to the plan update page. Calls self._click_with_instant_ink_expansion(self.update_plan_menu_link) which automatically handles mobile menu expansion and Instant Ink parent menu expansion if needed before clicking the Update Plan link.

Clicks the Print and Payment History menu item in the side navigation to navigate to the history page. Calls self._click_with_instant_ink_expansion(self.print_history_menu_link) which automatically handles mobile menu expansion and Instant Ink parent menu expansion if needed before clicking the Print History link.

Clicks the Shipment Tracking menu item in the side navigation to navigate to the shipment tracking page. Calls self._click_with_instant_ink_expansion(self.shipment_tracking_menu_link) which automatically handles mobile menu expansion and Instant Ink parent menu expansion if needed before clicking the Shipment Tracking link.

Clicks the Shipping & Billing submenu item under the Account section to navigate to the shipping and billing management page. Calls self._click_with_account_expansion(self.shipping_billing_submenu_link) which automatically handles expanding the Account parent menu if needed before clicking the Shipping & Billing link.

Clicks the Notifications submenu item under the Account section to navigate to the notifications preferences page. Calls self._click_with_account_expansion(self.notifications_submenu_link) which automatically handles expanding the Account parent menu if needed before clicking the Notifications link.

Expands the HP Instant Ink parent menu section to reveal its submenu items. First checks if the overview_menu_link is already visible using self.overview_menu_link.is_visible(). If not visible, it means the Instant Ink menu is collapsed. In that case, it tries to check if the mobile menu toggle is visible and clicks it if present (wrapped in try-except to handle desktop layout where this element doesn't exist). Then clicks the instant_ink_menu_link to expand the Instant Ink section. This method is useful when you need to verify submenu items are visible without clicking them.

Expands the Account parent menu section to reveal its submenu items. Checks if the shipping_billing_submenu_link is already visible using self.shipping_billing_submenu_link.is_visible(). If not visible, it means the Account menu is collapsed, so it clicks the my_account_menu_link to expand the Account section. This method is useful when you need to verify Account submenu items are visible without clicking them.

Waits for the HP Instant Ink menu link to become visible on the page with a very long timeout of 120000 milliseconds (2 minutes). Calls self.page.wait_for_selector() with the instant_ink_menu_link selector string from self.elements.instant_ink_menu_link, state='visible', and timeout=120000. Returns the element locator once it becomes visible. This method is useful for waiting for the dashboard side menu to fully load after login or page navigation, as the side menu may take time to render. The extended timeout suggests this is used in scenarios where page load may be slow.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This page object demonstrates sophisticated menu navigation handling with automatic expansion logic for nested menus and responsive mobile layouts. The class uses three levels of helper methods: _click_with_parent_expansion for basic parent-child menu expansion, _click_with_instant_ink_expansion for Instant Ink submenu items with mobile menu handling, and _click_with_account_expansion for Account submenu items. The public click methods (click_overview, click_update_plan, etc.) provide clean interfaces for test scripts while hiding the complexity of menu expansion logic. The page handles both desktop and mobile layouts automatically by checking for the mobile menu toggle visibility. The visible_instant_ink_menu_link method has an unusually long 120-second timeout, suggesting it's used for initial page load waits. All locators use data-testid attributes with descriptive names following the pattern 'jarvis-react-consumer-layout__side-menu-item-{MenuName}'. The class sets a default timeout of 30 seconds in the constructor. This is a well-designed page object that encapsulates complex navigation logic and provides simple, reliable methods for test scripts.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the Gemini Admin page, which appears to be an administrative interface for managing subscriptions and payments. Contains locators for viewing item details and performing refund operations. This is a minimal page object focused on admin-specific functionality.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates UI elements for the Gemini Admin interface. Provides locators for viewing item details in card or definition list format and for accessing the refund payment functionality. This appears to be an internal administrative tool for HP support or operations staff to manage customer subscriptions and process refunds. The minimal set of locators suggests this page object is focused on specific admin tasks rather than comprehensive page coverage.

locators.ui_element_description: Title or header elements for items displayed in the admin interface. Uses a compound selector to match either definition list term elements (dl dt) or card header elements (.card-header), suggesting the admin interface can display information in multiple formats.

Content or body elements for items displayed in the admin interface. Uses a compound selector to match either definition list description elements (dl dd) or card body elements (.card-body), corresponding to the content sections that pair with the base_item_title headers.

Link or button to initiate a payment refund for a member. Uses a child combinator selector to find an anchor element inside an element with class 'refund_payment_member_link'. This is likely used by admin staff to process refunds for customer subscriptions.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the GeminiAdminPage by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page). Then defines three UI element locators as attributes on the self.elements object. These include base_item_title for item headers using the selector 'dl dt, .card-header' to match either definition list terms or card headers, base_item_content for item content using 'dl dd, .card-body' to match either definition list descriptions or card bodies, and refund_payment_button for the refund action link using '.refund_payment_member_link > a' to find an anchor element within the refund link container. The locators use CSS selectors with compound selectors and child combinators for flexible element matching.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a minimal page object with only 3 locators and no action methods beyond the constructor. The name 'Gemini' likely refers to an internal HP admin tool or system. The locators suggest the page displays information in either definition list (dl/dt/dd) or card (.card-header/.card-body) formats, indicating flexible UI rendering. The refund_payment_button is the only interactive element defined, suggesting this page object is specifically focused on the refund workflow rather than comprehensive admin functionality. The lack of action methods means test scripts will interact with these locators directly or through BasePageObject methods. This page object would typically be used in admin or support test scenarios rather than end-user flows.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the HP ID authentication and account creation pages. Contains all UI element locators for the HP ID sign-in flow, account creation form, email verification, and country selection. Handles both sign-in and sign-up user journeys for HP ID authentication system.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements for HP ID authentication pages including sign-in, account creation, and email verification flows. Provides locators for form input fields (first name, last name, email, password), validation helper text elements, submit buttons, country selector, and sign-in options. Covers both the initial sign-in page and the account creation/registration page. Used for automating HP ID authentication flows which are prerequisites for accessing HP Instant Ink services.

locators.ui_element_description: Helper text element that appears below the first name input field, typically showing validation errors or guidance messages when the first name field has invalid input or is required but empty.
Helper text element that appears below the last name input field, typically showing validation errors or guidance messages when the last name field has invalid input or is required but empty.
Helper text element that appears below the email input field, typically showing validation errors like 'Invalid email format' or 'Email already exists' or guidance messages.
Helper text element that appears below the password input field, typically showing validation errors like 'Password must be at least 8 characters' or password strength requirements.
Submit button on the account creation form that submits the registration form with user details to create a new HP ID account.

Button element within the country selector container that opens the country selection dropdown or modal. Uses a descendant selector to find button elements inside the country-selector-buttons-container.
Username or email input field on the HP ID sign-in page where users enter their HP ID email address or username to begin the authentication process.

Submit button for the username form that advances to the password entry step after entering the username/email. This is part of a multi-step sign-in flow where username and password are entered on separate screens.
Password input field on the HP ID sign-in page where users enter their password after submitting their username. This is the second step in the multi-step sign-in flow.
Sign in submit button that completes the authentication process after entering the password. Clicking this button submits the credentials and logs the user into their HP ID account.

Sign in button option on the HP ID value proposition page. Uses a descendant selector to find the sign-in-button within the hpid-value-prop container. This appears on pages where users can choose to sign in instead of creating a new account.
First name input field on the HP ID account creation form where users enter their first name during registration.
Last name input field on the HP ID account creation form where users enter their last name during registration.
Email input field on the HP ID account creation form where users enter their email address which will become their HP ID username.
Market or country selection dropdown field on the account creation form where users select their geographic market or country.
Submit button for the email verification code form. Used to submit the verification code that was sent to the user's email address during account creation.
Verification code input field where users enter the numeric or alphanumeric code received via email to verify their email address during account creation.

Create account button using a compound selector that matches either the data-testid='create-account-button' or the ID #sign-up. This provides a fallback selector for the account creation submit button across different page versions.
HP ID value proposition page container element. This page typically explains the benefits of creating an HP ID account and offers options to sign in or create an account.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the HPIDPage by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page). Then defines 15 UI element locators as attributes on the self.elements object covering the complete HP ID authentication flow. These include validation helper text elements (firstName_helper_text, lastName_helper_text, email_helper_text, password_helper_text) for form validation messages, form input fields (firstName, lastName, email, market for account creation, username and password for sign-in, code for email verification), submit buttons (create_account_button, user_name_form_submit, sign_in, submit_code, create_account_button_data), navigation elements (country_selector_button, sign_in_option), and page containers (hpid_value_prop_page). The locators use a mix of ID selectors (#element-id), data-testid attributes, and compound selectors for flexibility across different page versions.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This page object covers the complete HP ID authentication flow including sign-in, account creation, and email verification. It has 15 locators but no action methods, meaning test scripts will interact with these elements directly or through BasePageObject methods. The page handles a multi-step sign-in flow where username and password are entered separately (username -> user_name_form_submit -> password -> sign_in). The account creation flow includes first name, last name, email, market selection, and email verification with a code. There are multiple locators for similar purposes (create_account_button vs create_account_button_data, sign_in vs sign_in_option) suggesting the page has evolved over time or supports multiple UI versions. The helper text locators are useful for validation testing to verify error messages appear correctly. All locators primarily use ID selectors which are reliable but may be less stable than data-testid attributes if the page HTML structure changes.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the HP Instant Ink public landing page. Contains comprehensive UI element locators for all sections of the marketing landing page including header, hero section, how it works, plan selection, FAQs, go green section, refer-a-friend, paper delivery, footer, country selector, alert banners, and enrollment kit banner. Provides methods for interacting with plan cards and accessing specific page sections. This is the main entry point page for users discovering HP Instant Ink service.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements for the HP Instant Ink public landing page, which is the main marketing and information page for the service. Provides over 80 locators covering every section of the landing page including header navigation (sign in, sign up, country picker, mobile menu), hero section with get started button, how it works section with step descriptions, plan selection section with plan cards and navigation arrows, FAQs with tabs for different plan types, go green section, refer-a-friend section, paper delivery add-on section, footer links, country selector modal, alert banners, and enrollment kit banner. Also provides property methods for accessing specific elements and methods for interacting with plan cards. This is the most comprehensive page object in the framework, reflecting the complexity of the marketing landing page.

locators.ui_element_description: Sign in button in the page header that navigates users to the HP ID sign-in page. This is the primary authentication entry point for existing users.
Sign up button in the page header that navigates users to the account creation or enrollment flow. This is the primary call-to-action for new users in the header.

HP Instant Ink logo in the page header. Uses partial class match with ^= operator to find elements with class starting with 'globalHeader-module_newIiLogo'. Clicking this typically returns users to the landing page home.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the LandingPage by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page). Then defines 87 UI element locators as attributes on the self.elements object, covering every section of the HP Instant Ink landing page. These locators are organized by section: header elements (sign_in_button, sign_up_header_button, header_logo, header_plan, flag_icon, header_section, country_picker, manage_account_button, hamburger_menu), mobile menu links (mobile_sign_in_link, mobile_sign_up_link, mobile_how_it_works_link, mobile_instant_paper_link, mobile_plans_link, mobile_faqs_link), hero section (hero_section, get_started_button), support elements (virtual_agent, virtual_agent_fixed_button, support_section, support_phone_number), how it works section (how_it_works, how_it_works_never_run_out, how_it_works_save_time_money, how_it_works_plan, how_it_works_ship, how_it_works_flexibility, when_we_save), plan section (plan_banner, plan_section, ink_plans_tab, landing_page_plans_container, ink_plans_tab_content, need_more_plan_info, plans_info_modal, plans_info_modal_signin_link, modal_close, check_printer_eligibility_link, eligibility_search_modal, plan_sign_up_now_button, ink_plans_card_right_arrow, ink_plans_card_left_arrow), FAQ section (faqs_section, show_more_button, faq_general_tab, faq_monthly_plans_tab, faq_yearly_plans_tab, faq_pay_as_you_print_tab), go green section (go_green_section, go_green_learn_more), refer-a-friend section (raf_section, raf_learn_more, raf_banner, raf_footnote), paper delivery section (paper_delivery_section, paper_delivery_title, paper_delivery_description, paper_delivery_image, paper_delivery_learn_more_link, paper_delivery_superscript, combined_savings_footnote), business section (instant_ink_for_business_section), footer elements (footnotes_section, footer_section, footer_hp_link, footer_privacy_statement, footer_terms_of_use, footer_cookies, footer_copyright, footer_privacy_choice, footer_iitos, terms_of_service_page), country selector (country_selector_modal, country_selector_title, country_selector_items), alert banners (alert_banner, alert_banner_container, alert_banner_icon, alert_banner_content, alert_banner_link, alert_banner_close, emergency_banner_header, alert_banner_title, alert_banner_subtitle, alert_banner_body, alert_banner_hide_link, alert_banner_show_link, alert_banner_dismiss_button), and enrollment kit banner (ek_banner, ek_banner_content, ek_message_container, ek_banner_message, ek_signup_link). The locators use a mix of data-testid attributes, ID selectors, class selectors with partial matching, and complex CSS selectors with descendant combinators and pseudo-classes.

parameters.name: card
parameters.description: The card identifier or index to locate a specific plan card

functions.what_it_does: Returns a Playwright locator for the pages container of a specific ink plan card. Constructs a dynamic selector by interpolating the card parameter into the selector string using an f-string: f"[data-testid='landing-page-plans-container'] [data-testid^='lp-plans-plan-card-{card}'] [class^='styles__PagesContainer-']". This finds the pages container within a specific plan card within the landing page plans container. The method uses page.locator() to create and return the locator object. This is useful for interacting with the page indicators or pagination controls within a specific plan card.

parameters.name: card
parameters.description: The card identifier or index to locate a specific plan card

functions.what_it_does: Returns a Playwright locator for a specific ink plan card. Constructs a dynamic selector by interpolating the card parameter into the selector string using an f-string: f"[data-testid='landing-page-plans-container'] [data-testid='lp-plans-plan-card-{card}'] [class^='styles__PagesContainer-']". This finds the pages container within a specific plan card identified by the card parameter. The method uses page.locator() to create and return the locator object. This allows test scripts to interact with specific plan cards dynamically based on the card identifier.

Property method that returns the first flag icon element on the page. Uses self.page.locator(self.elements.flag_icon) to create a locator for all flag icon elements, then calls .first to return only the first matching element. This is useful when there are multiple flag icons on the page but you only need to interact with the first one, typically the one in the header. The @property decorator allows this method to be accessed like an attribute without parentheses.

Property method that returns the first go green section element on the page. Uses self.page.locator(self.elements.go_green_section) to create a locator for all go green section elements, then calls .first to return only the first matching element. This ensures that even if there are multiple elements with the same selector, only the first one is returned. The @property decorator allows this method to be accessed like an attribute without parentheses.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is the largest and most comprehensive page object in the batch with 87 locators covering every section of the HP Instant Ink landing page. The page object demonstrates several advanced patterns: property methods with @property decorator for accessing specific elements (flag_icon, go_green_section), dynamic locator methods that accept parameters to construct selectors at runtime (ink_plans_tab_pages, ink_plans_card), and extensive use of data-testid attributes for reliable element identification. The page handles multiple UI versions with duplicate locators for the same logical element (e.g., how_it_works defined twice, alert_banner_hide_link and alert_banner_show_link using the same selector). The locators are well-organized by page section with clear naming conventions. Many locators use partial class matching with ^= or *= operators to handle CSS module naming patterns. The page includes comprehensive coverage of alert banners with separate locators for legacy banners, emergency banners, and enrollment kit banners. The FAQ section has separate tab locators for different plan types. The mobile menu has separate locators from desktop header elements. This page object would be the primary entry point for testing the public-facing marketing and information aspects of HP Instant Ink. The lack of action methods beyond the constructor and property getters suggests that test scripts interact with these elements directly or through BasePageObject methods.

DashboardSideMenuPage.click_update_plan — Navigate to plan update page
DashboardSideMenuPage.click_print_history — Navigate to print and payment history
DashboardSideMenuPage.click_shipping_billing — Navigate to shipping and billing management

LandingPage.ink_plans_tab_pages — Get pages container for specific plan card
LandingPage.ink_plans_card — Get specific plan card locator
LandingPage.flag_icon — Get first flag icon element
LandingPage.go_green_section — Get first go green section element
key_reusable_locators: DashboardHPSmartPage.signup_now_button — Sign up button for HP Instant Ink enrollment
DashboardHPSmartPage.update_plan_link — Link to update subscription plan
DashboardHPSmartPage.remove_printer_link — Link to remove printer from subscription
DashboardSideMenuPage.instant_ink_menu_link — HP Instant Ink parent menu item
HPIDPage.firstName — First name input for account creation
HPIDPage.email — Email input for account creation
LandingPage.plan_sign_up_now_button — Plan card sign up button
LandingPage.footer_terms_of_use — Footer terms of use link
